changes.check: Stage 2B — Determinism risk: stateful object initialization
changes.citation: backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py lines 115-129: AnalysisOrchestrator.__init__ initialises 12 stateful engine objects (ScoringEngine, SignalRegistry, ClusterEngineV2, InsightSynthesizer, etc.). Phase decomposition into named phase modules must not alter construction order, import-time side effects, or scope of these objects. Shared state across phases is the primary determinism risk. Cursor must confirm all stateful objects remain in __init__ or equivalent single-owner scope and are not duplicated or re-instantiated across phase modules.

changes.citation: 5 new classes required (prompt lines 667-677): orchestrator_phase_output_changed, pipeline_phase_regression, scaffold_documentation_missing_for_new_pattern, active_signal_biomarker_missing_ssot_metadata, ssot_metadata_unreviewed_for_kb_wave_target. Each must point to an active deterministic test. No placeholder entries. If Scope A splits, Sentinel obligations must adjust and be documented explicitly.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. risk_level HIGH and change_type MIXED correctly classified. All 9 mandatory authority paths confirmed present on disk. All 10 cross-sprint regression test files confirmed present. All 21 Tier 1 biomarkers confirmed in SSOT — all metadata fields empty and real population work exists. Orchestrator confirmed as single entry point with complex stateful initialisation — decomposition determinism risk surfaced. Two warnings: (1) Scope C schema gap — clinical_caveats/directionality_caveat fields absent from biomarkers.yaml; Cursor must check and STOP-to-propose-schema-extension if needed during Scope C inventory. (2) Stateful object initialisation in orchestrator.__init__ — phase decomposition must not alter construction order or scope. Zero blockers. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start.
